option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# ⚡ GitHub Actions Quick Start (5 Minutes)
## The Short Answer
**Yes, this bot can run automatically on GitHub Actions!** ✅
No more keeping your PC on. GitHub's servers run it for you on a schedule.
---
## Quick Setup (Copy-Paste)
### 1. Push to GitHub ```bash git push origin main ```
### 2. Add Secrets (GitHub UI) Go to: **Your Repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret**
Add these: ``` GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL = your-email@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY = -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY----- GOOGLE_SHEETS_API_KEY = your-key Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY = your-key Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL = https://api.qwebsite.com ```
### 3. Done! ✅ The workflow file is already at: `.github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml`
It will run automatically on: - **1st of every month at 10:00 AM UTC** - **15th of every month at 10:00 AM UTC**
---
## Change Schedule
Edit `.github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml` line 13:
```yaml - cron: "0 10 1,15 * *" # Change this line ```
Common examples: - Every day: `"0 10 * * *"` - Every week: `"0 10 * * 0"` - Every 6 hours: `"0 */6 * * *"` - 4 times/month: `"0 10 1,8,15,22 * *"`
Use https://crontab.guru to test your schedule
---
## Test It Now
1. Go to: **GitHub → Actions tab** 2. Select: **"Scheduled Automation - Bot Execution"** 3. Click: **"Run workflow"** 4. Set options and click **"Run workflow"** 5. Watch logs in real-time 6. Download reports after completion
---
## Where Are My Reports?
1. **GitHub Actions → Latest Run → Artifacts** 2. Download `automation-reports-{NUMBER}.zip` 3. Extract and open HTML files
---
## Common Questions
### Q: Will it really run without my PC on? **A:** Yes! GitHub's servers run it in the cloud.
### Q: What if credentials expire? **A:** Update the secret in GitHub Settings → Secrets, and it's instantly live.
### Q: Can I run it more often? **A:** Yes! Edit the cron, or manually trigger anytime via "Run workflow"
### Q: What if it fails? **A:** Check logs under Actions tab. Add `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL` secret for Slack notifications.
### Q: Is it free? **A:** Yes! 2,000 free minutes/month per public repo. Your schedule uses ~30 min/month.
---
## Next: Full Setup
For complete details, see: [`GITHUB_ACTIONS_SETUP.md`](./GITHUB_ACTIONS_SETUP.md)
---
**TL;DR: You're done!** Workflow is auto-configured and running on schedule.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# GitHub Actions Setup Guide
## Overview This guide helps you set up automatic scheduled runs of your QWebsite Automation Bot on GitHub Actions (2-4 times per month).
**Benefits:** - ✅ No need to keep your PC on - ✅ Automatic scheduling (1st and 15th of every month at 10 AM UTC) - ✅ Secure credential management with GitHub Secrets - ✅ Automatic report storage and artifact retention - ✅ Optional Slack/Email notifications - ✅ Manual trigger option for testing
---
## Step 1: Prepare Your Repository
### 1a. Check Git Repository Ensure your project is in a GitHub repository (not just local):
```bash git remote -v # Output should show GitHub URL, e.g., https://github.com/yourusername/AestheticRxNetworkIntelligentBot ```
If not on GitHub yet: ```bash git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/AestheticRxNetworkIntelligentBot.git git branch -M main git push -u origin main ```
### 1b. Workflow File Location The GitHub Actions workflow is located at: ``` .github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml ```
---
## Step 2: Add GitHub Secrets
GitHub Actions uses **Secrets** to securely store credentials. They're encrypted and never exposed in logs.
### How to Add Secrets:
1. Go to: **GitHub Repository → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** 2. Click **"New repository secret"** 3. Add each secret one by one:
### Required Secrets
#### Google Authentication (Required) ``` Name: GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL Value: your-service-account@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com ```
``` Name: GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY Value: -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY----- ...YOUR_PRIVATE_KEY_HERE... -----END PRIVATE KEY----- (Paste the ENTIRE key including BEGIN/END lines) ```
``` Name: GOOGLE_SHEETS_API_KEY Value: your-api-key-here ```
#### Q Website API (Required) ``` Name: Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY Value: your-api-key ```
``` Name: Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL Value: https://api.qwebsite.com (or your actual URL) ```
#### Bitwarden (Optional - only if using Bitwarden for credentials) ``` Name: BW_CLIENTID Value: your-client-id ```
``` Name: BW_CLIENTSECRET Value: your-client-secret ```
``` Name: BW_USERNAME Value: your-bitwarden-username ```
``` Name: BW_PASSWORD Value: your-bitwarden-password ```
#### Gmail (Optional - only if using email reader) ``` Name: GMAIL_USER Value: your-gmail@gmail.com ```
``` Name: GMAIL_PASSWORD Value: your-app-password (NOT regular password) ```
#### Slack Webhook (Optional - for notifications) ``` Name: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL Value: https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK/URL ```
#### Process Control (Optional - to enable specific processes) ``` Name: RUN_UPDATE_PAYMENT_PROCESS Value: False ```
``` Name: RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM Value: True ```
``` Name: RUN_DATA_ANALYSIS_PROCESS Value: False ```
And so on for other processes...
---
## Step 3: Customize the Schedule
The workflow runs on the **1st and 15th of every month at 10:00 UTC**.
To change the schedule, edit `.github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml`:
```yaml on: schedule: - cron: "0 10 1,15 * *" # Current: 1st and 15th at 10:00 UTC ```
### Common Cron Examples:
| Schedule | Cron | Notes | |----------|------|-------| | **Twice a month** | `0 10 1,15 * *` | 1st & 15th at 10 AM UTC | | **Weekly** | `0 10 * * 0` | Every Sunday at 10 AM UTC | | **Daily** | `0 10 * * *` | Every day at 10 AM UTC | | **Every 6 hours** | `0 */6 * * *` | 4 times per day | | **4 times a month** | `0 10 1,8,15,22 * *` | 1st, 8th, 15th, 22nd |
**Timezone Note:** Cron times are always in UTC. Convert your timezone: - UTC-5 (EST): Add 5 hours - UTC-8 (PST): Add 8 hours - UTC+1 (CET): Subtract 1 hour
---
## Step 4: Test the Workflow
### Manual Trigger Test:
1. Go to: **GitHub Repository → Actions** 2. Select: **"Scheduled Automation - Bot Execution"** 3. Click: **"Run workflow"** 4. Choose settings: - `run_all_processes`: true/false - `run_order_management`: true/false - `run_data_analysis`: true/false 5. Click: **"Run workflow"**
### Monitor Execution:
1. Go to **Actions** tab 2. Click the running workflow 3. Expand **"Execute QWebsite Automation Bot"** job 4. View real-time logs
---
## Step 5: View & Download Reports
### Option A: Via GitHub UI
1. Go to: **Actions → Latest workflow run** 2. Scroll to: **"Artifacts"** section 3. Download: `automation-reports-{RUN_NUMBER}.zip` 4. Extract to view HTML reports
### Option B: Via Command Line
```bash # List all workflow runs gh run list --workflow=scheduled-automation.yml
# Download latest artifacts gh run download <RUN_ID> -D ./downloaded_reports/ ```
### Option C: Automatic Upload to Google Drive (Advanced)
The workflow can upload reports to Google Drive. Implementation:
```bash # Install Google Drive CLI pip install google-drive-api
# Add to workflow or extend upload step ```
---
## Step 6: Optional - Add Notifications
### Slack Notifications
1. Create a Slack Webhook: - Go to: https://api.slack.com/apps - Create new app → "From scratch" - Name: "QWebsite Bot" - Select your workspace - Enable: **"Incoming Webhooks"** - Click: **"Add New Webhook to Workspace"** - Select channel: #bot-notifications - Copy webhook URL
2. Add to GitHub Secrets: `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL`
3. Workflow will auto-notify on failures
### Email Notifications
GitHub provides native email notifications for workflow failures: 1. Settings → Notifications → Check "Email notifications" 2. Or configure per-workflow in **Actions** settings
---
## Step 7: Troubleshooting
### ❌ Workflow fails with "ModuleNotFoundError"
**Solution:** ```bash # Ensure dependencies are in setup.cfg or pyproject.toml uv pip install -e . --system # Reinstalls with latest deps ```
### ❌ Credentials not loading
**Solution:** - Verify secrets are added in **Settings → Secrets** - Check secret names match exactly (case-sensitive) - Re-test with `workflow_dispatch` (manual trigger)
### ❌ API calls fail with 401/403
**Solution:** - Verify API keys in secrets are current - Check service account has required permissions - Verify Google Sheets shared with service account email: `googledrive@qwebsite.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
### ❌ Private key parsing errors
**Solution:** - Ensure full private key is pasted (including `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`) - Use raw key format (no extra quotes or escaping) - Verify it's a newline-separated format in the secret
### ❌ Workflow runs but reports are empty
**Solution:** - Check logs in Actions tab for errors - Enable debug logging: Add secret `ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG: true` - Run manual test with verbose output
---
## Monitoring & Maintenance
### Weekly Check
```bash # Check latest workflow status gh run list --workflow=scheduled-automation.yml --limit=5
# View failed runs gh run list --workflow=scheduled-automation.yml --status=failure ```
### Monthly Review
- Review generated reports in artifacts - Check for any API errors or failures - Rotate credentials annually - ⏰ Adjust cron schedule if needed
### Update Dependencies
```bash # Periodically update dependencies uv pip compile pyproject.toml -o requirements.txt # Then commit and push ```
---
## Advanced: Parallel Workspaces
Run different configurations simultaneously:
```yaml jobs: order-management: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM=True python task.py
data-analysis: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: RUN_DATA_ANALYSIS_PROCESS=True python task.py
# Both jobs run in parallel, outputs merged ```
---
## Cost Implications
**GitHub Actions Pricing (as of 2024):** - Free tier: 2,000 minutes/month on public repos - Each bot run: ~5-15 minutes (depending on data size) - **Cost for 2×/month: FREE** ✅
---
## Next Steps
1. ✅ Push `.github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml` to GitHub 2. ✅ Add all required secrets in Repository Settings 3. ✅ Test with `workflow_dispatch` (manual trigger) 4. ✅ Verify reports generate correctly 5. ✅ Adjust cron schedule if needed 6. ✅ Enable notifications (optional) 7. ✅ Monitor first automated run on scheduled date
---
## Support & Resources
- **GitHub Actions Docs:** https://github.com/features/actions - **Cron Schedule Helper:** https://crontab.guru - **Robocorp Docs:** https://robocorp.com/docs
---
**You're all set!** Your bot will now run automatically without needing your PC on.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# GitHub Actions Workflow Templates
This file contains alternative workflow configurations for different automation scenarios.
---
## Template 1: Order Management Only (Weekly)
Use this if you want to run **only order management** every week.
Save as: `.github/workflows/order-management-weekly.yml`
```yaml name: Weekly Order Management
on: schedule: - cron: "0 10 * * 0" # Every Sunday at 10 AM UTC
jobs: order-sync: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: "3.11" cache: "pip" - run: pip install uv - run: uv pip install -e . --system - name: Run Order Management run: python task.py env: RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM: "True" RUN_UPDATE_PAYMENT_PROCESS: "False" RUN_USER_MANAGEMENT_PROCESS: "False" RUN_ADVERTISEMENT_MANAGEMENT_PROCESS: "False" RUN_SIGNUP_ID_MANAGEMENT_PROCESS: "False" RUN_DATA_ANALYSIS_PROCESS: "False" GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL }} GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL }} - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 if: always() with: name: order-reports-${{ github.run_number }} path: output/ retention-days: 30 ```
---
## Template 2: Business Report Only (Monthly)
Use this to generate **business reports** on the 1st of every month.
Save as: `.github/workflows/business-report-monthly.yml`
```yaml name: Monthly Business Report
on: schedule: - cron: "0 10 1 * *" # 1st of every month at 10 AM UTC
jobs: generate-report: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: "3.11" cache: "pip" - run: pip install uv - run: uv pip install -e . --system - name: Generate Business Report run: python task.py env: RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM: "False" RUN_DATA_ANALYSIS_PROCESS: "True" # This runs BusinessReport automatically! RUN_BUSINESS_REPORT_PROCESS: "True" GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL }} GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY }} GOOGLE_SHEETS_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SHEETS_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL }} - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 if: always() with: name: business-reports-${{ github.run_number }} path: output/business_report_*.html retention-days: 90 - name: Notify on Slack if: always() uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1.24.0 with: payload: | { "text": " Monthly Business Report Generated", "blocks": [{ "type": "section", "text": { "type": "mrkdwn", "text": "*Report Status:* ${{ job.status }}\n*Run:* ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}" } }] } env: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }} SLACK_WEBHOOK_TYPE: INCOMING_WEBHOOK ```
---
## Template 3: All Processes Daily
Use this to run **all automation processes** every day.
Save as: `.github/workflows/full-automation-daily.yml`
```yaml name: Daily Full Automation
on: schedule: - cron: "0 02 * * *" # Every day at 2 AM UTC
jobs: full-automation: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: "3.11" cache: "pip" - run: pip install uv - run: uv pip install -e . --system - name: Run All Processes run: python task.py env: RUN_UPDATE_PAYMENT_PROCESS: ${{ secrets.RUN_UPDATE_PAYMENT_PROCESS || 'False' }} RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM: "True" RUN_USER_MANAGEMENT_PROCESS: "True" RUN_ADVERTISEMENT_MANAGEMENT_PROCESS: "True" RUN_SIGNUP_ID_MANAGEMENT_PROCESS: "True" RUN_DATA_ANALYSIS_PROCESS: "True" # All credentials... GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL }} GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY }} GOOGLE_SHEETS_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SHEETS_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL }} BW_CLIENTID: ${{ secrets.BW_CLIENTID }} BW_CLIENTSECRET: ${{ secrets.BW_CLIENTSECRET }} BW_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.BW_USERNAME }} BW_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.BW_PASSWORD }} - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 if: always() with: name: full-automation-${{ github.run_number }} path: output/ retention-days: 30 ```
---
## Template 4: Payment Updates + Order Management (Twice Weekly)
Use this for synchronized payment and order updates.
Save as: `.github/workflows/payment-order-sync.yml`
```yaml name: Payment & Order Synchronization
on: schedule: - cron: "0 09 * * 1,4" # Every Monday & Thursday at 9 AM UTC
jobs: sync-payments-orders: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: "3.11" cache: "pip" - run: pip install uv - run: uv pip install -e . --system - name: Sync Payments & Orders run: python task.py env: RUN_UPDATE_PAYMENT_PROCESS: "True" RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM: "True" RUN_DATA_ANALYSIS_PROCESS: "False" PAYMENT_IDS_LIST: ${{ secrets.PAYMENT_IDS_LIST }} GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL }} GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL }} - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 if: always() with: name: sync-reports-${{ github.run_number }} path: output/ retention-days: 30 ```
---
## Template 5: With Email Report Attachment
Use this to **email HTML reports** after completion.
```yaml # Add this step after artifact upload:
- name: Send Report Email if: success() uses: dawidd6/action-send-mail@v3 with: server_address: ${{ secrets.MAIL_SERVER }} server_port: ${{ secrets.MAIL_PORT }} username: ${{ secrets.MAIL_USERNAME }} password: ${{ secrets.MAIL_PASSWORD }} subject: "QWebsite Automation Report - ${{ github.run_id }}" to: ${{ secrets.REPORT_EMAIL_TO }} from: ${{ secrets.MAIL_FROM }} body: | Automation Report Generated
Build: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
Reports attached. attachments: | output/business_report_*.html output/workflow_report_*.html ```
---
## Template 6: With Dependency Updates Check
Use this to **automatically update dependencies** and test the bot.
```yaml name: Dependency Update & Test
on: schedule: - cron: "0 00 1 * *" # 1st of every month at midnight UTC
jobs: update-deps: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: "3.11" - name: Update dependencies run: | pip install --upgrade pip uv uv pip compile pyproject.toml -o requirements.txt --upgrade git config user.email "bot@github.com" git config user.name "GitHub Bot" git add requirements.txt pyproject.toml git commit -m "chore: update dependencies" || echo "No changes" git push - name: Run tests run: | uv pip install -e . --system pytest -v - name: Run automation if: success() run: python task.py env: RUN_ORDER_MANAGE_SYSTEM: "True" GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL }} GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY }} Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL }} ```
---
## How to Use These Templates
1. Choose a template from above that matches your needs 2. Save it to `.github/workflows/` with the suggested filename 3. Update secrets references to match your actual secrets 4. Commit and push to GitHub 5. It will run automatically on the schedule!
---
## Combining Multiple Workflows
You can have **multiple workflows running in parallel**:
``` .github/workflows/ ├── scheduled-automation.yml (Twice monthly: Order + Analysis) ├── order-management-weekly.yml (Weekly: Order sync only) ├── business-report-monthly.yml (Monthly: Full report) └── payment-order-sync.yml (Twice weekly: Payments) ```
Each runs independently on its own schedule!
---
## Tips
- **Timezone Conversion:** Use https://crontab.guru - **Monitor Runs:** GitHub Actions → Analytics tab - **Debug Mode:** Add secret `ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG: true` - **Keep Logs:** Adjust `retention-days: 30` as needed - **Email Reports:** Add `dawidd6/action-send-mail@v3` step - **Slack Alerts:** Add webhook to get notified instantly
---

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# ✅ Verification & Troubleshooting Checklist
Use this guide to verify your GitHub Actions setup is working correctly and troubleshoot any issues.
---
## ✅ Pre-Launch Verification
Before the first scheduled run, verify everything is configured:
### 1. Repository on GitHub - [ ] Repository is on GitHub (not local only) - [ ] `.github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml` file exists - [ ] Run: `git log --oneline | head -3` shows commits are pushed
### 2. GitHub Secrets Configured - [ ] `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` is set - [ ] `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` is set (full key with -----BEGIN/END-----) - [ ] `Q_WEBSITE_API_KEY` is set - [ ] `Q_WEBSITE_API_BASE_URL` is set - [ ] Optional secrets (Bitwarden, Gmail, Slack) set if needed
**Verify:** Go to **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** → should see all secrets listed
### 3. Workflow File Syntax - [ ] Run locally to check YAML syntax:
```bash # Install yamllint pip install yamllint
# Check syntax yamllint .github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml # Should output: "✅ No errors" or be empty ```
### 4. Python Dependencies - [ ] `uv` is in your dependencies or `pyproject.toml` - [ ] `pyproject.toml` exists and is valid - [ ] Run locally: `uv pip install -e .` works without errors
---
## Test Workflow (Manual Trigger)
### Step 1: Trigger Manually 1. Go to: **GitHub → Actions tab** 2. Find: **"Scheduled Automation - Bot Execution"** workflow 3. Click: **"Run workflow"** button 4. Select options (or use defaults) 5. Click: **"Run workflow"**
### Step 2: Monitor Execution 1. Click the workflow name (in the list) 2. Click the job: **"Execute QWebsite Automation Bot"** 3. Expand each step and verify: - ✅ `Checkout repository` - should pass immediately - ✅ `Set up Python 3.11` - should pass in ~10 sec - ✅ `Install uv` - should pass in ~5 sec - ✅ `Install dependencies` - should pass in ~30 sec - ✅ `Setup environment variables` - should pass immediately - ✅ `Run QWebsite Automation Bot` - main step, watch logs - ✅ `Upload execution reports` - reports saved
### Step 3: Review Logs If any step fails, click the failed step to see error details:
``` ❌ ERROR: Module not found: `requests` Solution: Add to pyproject.toml under [project] dependencies ```
---
## Download & Verify Reports
### Via GitHub UI 1. Go to: **Actions → Latest workflow run** 2. Scroll down: **Artifacts** section 3. Download: `automation-reports-{NUMBER}.zip` 4. Extract: `unzip automation-reports-*.zip` 5. Verify files exist: - `business_report_*.html` (if data analysis ran) - `workflow_report_*.html` (always generated) - `output/exports/` (data exports)
### Via Command Line ```bash # List artifacts gh run list --workflow=scheduled-automation.yml --limit=3
# Download & extract RUN_ID=$(gh run list --workflow=scheduled-automation.yml --limit=1 --json=databaseId --jq='.[0].databaseId') gh run download $RUN_ID -D ./reports/ unzip reports/automation-reports-*.zip -d reports/ ```
---
## Troubleshooting Matrix
### ❌ Error: "No such file or directory: task.py"
**Cause:** Workflow running in wrong directory
**Solution:** ```bash # Verify file exists git ls-files | grep task.py
# If not there: git add task.py git commit -m "Add task.py" git push ```
---
### ❌ Error: "ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'"
**Cause:** Dependencies not installed
**Solution:** 1. Verify `pyproject.toml` exists at repo root 2. Check `PYTHONPATH` in workflow includes `src`: ```yaml env: PYTHONPATH: ".:src:workflow" ```
---
### ❌ Error: "401 Unauthorized" from Google API
**Cause:** Invalid credentials
**Verification steps:** ```bash # 1. Verify secret is set gh secret list | grep GOOGLE
# 2. Test locally with same secret export GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL="$(gh secret get GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL)" python task.py
# 3. If local works, workflow inherits secrets correctly ```
---
### ❌ Error: "Credentials refused (invalid)"
**Cause:** Private key formatting issue in GitHub Secret
**Solution:** ```bash # Get your private key file # Example: service-account-key.json
# Extract private key (Unix/Mac): jq -r '.private_key' service-account-key.json | sed 's/\\n/\n/g'
# Output should look like: # -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY----- # MIIEvQIBADANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQE... # -----END PRIVATE KEY-----
# Copy ENTIRE output (including BEGIN/END) to GitHub Secret # Do NOT add extra quotes ```
---
### ❌ Error: "Secret not found: GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY"
**Cause:** Secret name mismatch (case-sensitive)
**Solution:** ```bash # List all secrets gh secret list
# Add missing secret gh secret set GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY --body "$(cat key.txt)"
# Or via GitHub UI: # Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret ```
---
### ❌ Error: "Permission denied: cannot write to output/"
**Cause:** Artifact permissions issue (rare)
**Solution:** ```bash # In workflow, before running task: - name: Ensure output directory permissions run: mkdir -p output && chmod 755 output ```
---
### ❌ Workflow doesn't run on schedule
**Cause:** Repository might be archived, disabled, or cron not set
**Verification:** ```bash # 1. Verify schedule in workflow file cat .github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml | grep -A 3 "schedule:"
# 2. Check for typos in cron format # Should be: "minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week" # Example: "0 10 1,15 * *"
# 3. Use cron validator (browser) # Visit: https://crontab.guru/?0%2010%201,15%20*%20* ```
**Fix:** ```bash # Update schedule in workflow file # Edit .github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml git add .github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml git push # Wait for next scheduled time ```
---
### ❌ Artifacts upload fails
**Cause:** No report files generated
**Solution:** 1. Check bot execution logs (previous step) 2. Verify `output/` directory is created 3. Check file paths in upload step:
```yaml - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: path: output/ # Verify this path exists after run ```
---
## Debugging Mode
Enable detailed logging:
1. Go to: **Settings → Secrets → New repository secret** 2. Add: `ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG` = `true` 3. Re-run workflow 4. View logs with much more detail
---
## Performance Metrics
Track your workflow efficiency:
### Expected Run Times - **Checkout + Setup:** 30 seconds - **Install Dependencies:** 30-60 seconds - **Order Management Only:** 2-5 minutes - **Full Automation (all processes):** 10-20 minutes - **Upload Artifacts:** 10-30 seconds - **Total:** ~15-30 minutes
### If runs take longer than expected:
```bash # Check each step's duration gh run view <RUN_ID> --json=steps,conclusion,timer ```
---
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Never commit secrets to repository - [ ] Use `secrets.VAR_NAME` in workflow (not env files) - [ ] Rotate credentials every 6-12 months - [ ] Verify service accounts have minimal permissions - [ ] Check secret access logs (GitHub Enterprise only) - [ ] Use branch protection for workflow changes
---
## Verification Checklist (Pre-Production)
Before setting up the final schedule, complete this:
- [ ] Manual test passed (workflow_dispatch) - [ ] Reports generated successfully - [ ] No errors in logs - [ ] All secrets visible in `Settings → Secrets` - [ ] Cron schedule verified at crontab.guru - [ ] Email notifications configured (if using Slack) - [ ] Artifact retention set (30+ days) - [ ] Python 3.11+ installed on GitHub's servers ✅
---
## Still Not Working?
### Get Help 1. Check GitHub Actions workflow logs (most detailed) 2. Enable `ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG: true` secret 3. Run manually with `workflow_dispatch` to test 4. Compare your workflow against the template 5. Verify line endings are LF (not CRLF) in `.yml`
### Debug Commands ```bash # Validate YAML python -m yaml .github/workflows/scheduled-automation.yml
# Check Python syntax python -m py_compile task.py
# Test locally first python task.py
# Then push and test on GitHub: git push gh workflow run scheduled-automation.yml ```
---
## Common Support Scenarios
| Issue | Check | Fix | |-------|-------|-----| | Nothing runs | Cron schedule | Verify at crontab.guru | | Module not found | `pyproject.toml` | Add to dependencies | | API 401 error | Secrets | Verify in Settings tab | | No reports | Logs | Check bot execution step | | Takes too long | Performance | Run `Order Management Only` | | Out of storage | Artifacts | Reduce `retention-days` |
---
## ✅ Final Checklist
Before considering setup complete:
- [ ] Workflow file committed to main branch - [ ] All secrets added to GitHub Settings - [ ] Manual test passed with `workflow_dispatch` - [ ] Reports downloaded and verified - [ ] Cron schedule set correctly - [ ] First scheduled run has occurred (optional, wait for date) - [ ] Notifications working (Slack/Email, if configured) - [ ] Backup plan if GitHub Actions unavailable
---
**You're ready!** Your bot is now automated on GitHub Actions.
